steps.title: Spot the two-part requirement
steps.content: The strongest clue is the explicit requirement: the answer must both make a claim about Iberian orca behavior AND support it with evidence from the notes. No partial credit—both parts are essential.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A (‘The recent destructive behavior of Iberian orcas may be the result of understimulation, given that orcas' interactions with marine vessels have increased as the orcas have spent less time hunting.’) makes a clear causal claim—destructive behavior results from understimulation—and supports it directly by linking reduced hunting time to increased vessel interactions.

The other choices either lack a claim about orca behavior (C is purely descriptive), address a different topic entirely (B focuses on TAST) or contradict the notes about cause and effect (D reverses the tuna-hunting relationship).

That confirms A is correct.
